title: AI_OS Orchestration Control Room
summary: A beginner-readable view of the local queue, assigned agent, validator status, blocked actions, and next safe action.

cards.title: Active Queue
cards.meaning: One local work packet can be selected for review.

cards.title: Task State
cards.meaning: The task must pass ownership and blocked-action checks before work starts.
cards.next_action: Keep unclear tasks in review instead of APPLY.

cards.title: Blocked Action Locks
cards.meaning: Live trading, brokers, OANDA, secrets, real webhooks, real orders, background tasks, and startup persistence are blocked.
cards.next_action: Do not weaken the safety locks.

cards.title: Next Safe Action
cards.meaning: Run the read-only orchestration control room validator before commit planning.
cards.next_action: Run the local validator and review the result.

cards.title: Agent Ownership
cards.meaning: Every task must stay inside its assigned agent ownership boundary.

cards.title: Validator Status
cards.meaning: The validator checks local files and safety text only.
cards.next_action: Run validator after dashboard fixture changes.

cards.title: Runtime Health
cards.meaning: The runtime is a local control layer, not a background worker.

cards.title: Human Approval Gate
cards.meaning: The user must approve APPLY, commit, push, installs, integrations, and risky actions.
cards.next_action: Wait for explicit approval before risky steps.

next_safe_action: Run automation/agent_runtime/Test-AiOsOrchestrationControlRoom.DRY_RUN.ps1, then review the dashboard diff before any commit.
